methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $15M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

private.name: Stark Defence
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

private.name: Vatn Systems

private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

data.narrative:
SiFly is a Series A-stage defense & security company that has raised $15M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 firms—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private—including same-stage peers Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $15M raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, implies a $30M valuation. Peer-median analysis across three same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $300M, with a range of $150M to $500M. The wide dispersion reflects meaningful differences in technology maturity and capital deployment across the peer set.

Synthesizing both approaches produces a triangulated range of $26M to $345M, with a median of $165M. The 13-fold spread between methods reflects tension between stage-based progression (which anchors to current capital raised) and peer multiples (which incorporate market pricing for established Series A defense contractors). Key uncertainties include SiFly's technology differentiation relative to Stark Defence and Vatn Systems, revenue traction or near-term commercialization milestones, and whether peer valuations reflect recent market repricing in defense tech. A narrowed range would require clarity on near-term revenue visibility and technical validation against incumbents.
